verses.text: God bids him set his face toward Jerusalem and declare His sword drawn, to cut off 'the righteous and the wicked' alike. A general calamity. In national judgment the godly often share the outward stroke with the guilty.

He is to sigh before them with breaking heart, and when asked why, answer, For the tidings; every heart shall melt. Grief as a sign. The prophet's anguish foreshadows the terror that is coming.
He is to sigh before them with breaking heart, and when asked why, answer, For the tidings; every heart shall melt. Grief as a sign. The prophet's anguish foreshadows the terror that is coming.
A song of the sword: sharpened and polished 'to make a sore slaughter,' furbished to flash like lightning. Judgment readied. God's instruments of wrath are prepared with terrible exactness.
A song of the sword: sharpened and polished 'to make a sore slaughter,' furbished to flash like lightning. Judgment readied. God's instruments of wrath are prepared with terrible exactness.

He must cry and howl, for the sword falls on His people and their princes; 'it is a trial.' No exemption for rank. The testing reaches even those who thought themselves secure.
He must cry and howl, for the sword falls on His people and their princes; 'it is a trial.' No exemption for rank. The testing reaches even those who thought themselves secure.
Let him smite his hands; the sword is doubled and trebled, and God will make His fury rest. Relentless stroke. Repeated blows show the fixedness of the judgment.
Let him smite his hands; the sword is doubled and trebled, and God will make His fury rest. Relentless stroke. Repeated blows show the fixedness of the judgment.

He marks two roads for Babylon's sword, one to Jerusalem, one to Ammon. The way is appointed. God directs even an invader's route to His own ends.
He marks two roads for Babylon's sword, one to Jerusalem, one to Ammon. The way is appointed. God directs even an invader's route to His own ends.
He marks two roads for Babylon's sword, one to Jerusalem, one to Ammon. The way is appointed. God directs even an invader's route to His own ends.
The king pauses at the fork to seek omens, and the lot falls on Jerusalem. Heathen divination serves God's plan. The very superstitions of men are overruled to fulfil His word.

To the profane prince Ezekiel cries that his day is come; God will 'overturn, overturn, overturn it... until he come whose right it is.' Crown removed. The throne is unsettled until Christ, whose right it is, shall reign.

The sword is drawn against Ammon too, who shall be fuel for fire and 'no more remembered.' No mocker spared. Those who rejoiced at Judah's fall meet their own erasure.
